paper_title: The impact of the "National target program on new rural development" on household income: The case of Go Quao district, Kien Giang province

entities.description: Author from Kien Giang Organization Board of Party Committee of the Centrally-run Businesses’ Sector.
Author from the College of Economics, Can Tho University.

entities.id: Kien Giang Organization Board of Party Committee of the Centrally-run Businesses’ Sector
entities.description: Affiliation of the first author in Vietnam.

entities.id: College of Economics, Can Tho University

entities.description: The primary research field focusing on rural programs and household income.

entities.id: National Target Program on New Rural Development (NTP-NRD)
entities.description: A government program in Vietnam aimed at building new rural areas based on socio-economic criteria.
The primary outcome variable measuring the financial status of families in Go Quao district.
Resources such as physical and social capital that determine household income.
A statistical technique used to estimate the impact of a treatment by comparing the changes in outcomes over time between a treatment group and a control group.
A statistical model used to produce consistent estimates for panel data by handling correlations within households across years.
The data collection process conducted in 2015 at the study site.
Retrospective digital records of household income and socio-economic characteristics over a 5-year period.
The reported increase in income in the Mekong Delta by 2014.
The reduction in poverty observed in the Mekong Delta region.
The higher growth rate of income for treated households in the first year.
The estimated increase in income associated with being part of a cooperative.
The R-squared value or the extent to which independent variables explain the dependent variable.
Infrastructure metric achieved in the Mekong Delta.
